subtasks.description: Extract contract renewal dates and license details from SAM system document/records (contract ID, vendor, renewal date, current status)

Query consumption trends from SQL warehouse: aggregated usage metrics over last 6-12 months by contract/vendor, including utilization rate, cost per unit, trend direction (increasing/declining/stable)

Reconcile SAM renewal dates with consumption trends; assess renewal risk (imminent dates, high/low utilization, budget impact); classify renewals by urgency tier (critical, high, medium, low); generate renewal recommendations and notification payloads for procurement
Generate multi-tool notification workflow: format alerts by risk tier, route to procurement team via email/ticketing system, include renewal dates, consumption insights, and recommended actions

plan.cost_rationale: Four subtasks required: (1) SAM extraction is simple structured field retrieval (small). (2) Consumption trends require multi-table SQL aggregation with time-window analysis (mid tier). (3) Risk assessment combines two data sources (SAM + consumption), applies multi-signal logic (utilization vs. deadline vs. cost), and generates recommendations—this is complex reconciliation warranting frontier tier to handle nuanced urgency classification and decision logic. (4) Notification workflow requires multi-tool orchestration (email + ticketing + formatting) to reach procurement, assigned mid tier as the tool invocation is procedural rather than analytically complex. Total decomposition minimized: single extraction task (not per field), single SQL query (not separate metrics), consolidation of risk logic into one cross_recon step, and deferred notification to multitool_plan per enterprise tool registry pattern.

output.raw_output:
{"sql": "SELECT
  cf.publisher AS vendor_name,
  cf.product,
  cf.license_id AS contract_id,
  DATE_TRUNC('month', cf.event_date) AS month,
  SUM(cf.amount_usd) AS total_cost_usd,
  SUM(cf.daily_active_seats) AS total_active_seats,
  COUNT(DISTINCT cf.user_id) AS distinct_users,
  COUNT(cf.event_id) AS event_count,
  ROUND(SUM(cf.amount_usd) / NULLIF(SUM(cf.daily_active_seats), 0), 4) AS cost_per_seat,
  ROUND(
    100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT cf.user_id) / NULLIF(MAX(cf.daily_active_seats), 0),
    2
  ) AS utilization_rate_pct,
  ROUND(
    (SUM(cf.amount_usd) - LAG(SUM(cf.amount_usd), 1) OVER (
      PARTITION BY cf.publisher, cf.product, cf.license_id
      ORDER BY DATE_TRUNC('month', cf.event_date)
    )) / NULLIF(LAG(SUM(cf.amount_usd), 1) OVER (
      PARTITION BY cf.publisher, cf.product, cf.license_id
      ORDER BY DATE_TRUNC('month', cf.event_date)
    ), 0) * 100,
    2
  ) AS mom_cost_change_pct,
  CASE
    WHEN ROUND(
      (SUM(cf.amount_usd) - LAG(SUM(cf.amount_usd), 1) OVER (
        PARTITION BY cf.publisher, cf.product, cf.license_id
        ORDER BY DATE_TRUNC('month', cf.event_date)
      )) / NULLIF(LAG(SUM(cf.amount_usd), 1) OVER (
        PARTITION BY cf.publisher, cf.product, cf.license_id
        ORDER BY DATE_TRUNC('month', cf.event_date)
      ), 0) * 100,
      2
    ) > 5 THEN 'increasing'
    WHEN ROUND(
      (SUM(cf.amount_usd) - LAG(SUM(cf.amount_usd), 1) OVER (
        PARTITION BY cf.publisher, cf.product, cf.license_id
        ORDER BY DATE_TRUNC('month', cf.event_date)
      )) / NULLIF(LAG(SUM(cf.amount_usd), 1) OVER (
        PARTITION BY cf.publisher, cf.product, cf.license_id
        ORDER BY DATE_TRUNC('month', cf.event_date)
      ), 0) * 100,
      2
    ) < -5 THEN 'declining'
    ELSE 'stable'
  END AS trend_direction,
  MIN(cf.event_date) AS period_start,
  MAX(cf.event_date) AS
